summary: Read proofs/primitive_set_erdos.json end-to-end. State the claim, the three given facts (with their sign disambiguations), and the witness contract in your own words inside proof_strategy.md as Section 1 'Setup'. Do NOT attempt the proof yet.

Verify F3 numerically for k = 1, 2, 3, 4: compute the truncated sum over the first ~200 elements of A_k = {n: Omega(n) = k}, confirm each is < 1, and record the values + the leading correction term -(c+o(1)) k^2/2^k. Append a numerical evidence section to proof_strategy.md.

Compute the truncated sum over primes from 2 onwards (a primitive set), check it approaches ~1.6366, confirm this is consistent with F1 (which bounds the WHOLE primitive-set family at ~1.399 + o(1) but only for x -> infty; finite primes-from-2 sum is allowed to exceed 1.399). Document this distinction in proof_strategy.md.

Search for a primitive A in [x_floor, infty) with rigorously verified sum > 1.0 via library.primitive_set_witness.verify_witness. Try x_floor = 100, 1000, 10000. If found, embed as a <!-- WITNESS --> block in proof_strategy.md.

Outline a proof structure: stratify any primitive A by Omega(a). For each stratum bound the contribution. Write this as a sequence of lemmas in proof_lemmas/ (one file per lemma). Decide which lemmas are easy (cite F3-style estimates) and which are hard (the cross-stratum / non-omega-stratified case).

If after Q5 the proof structure has gaps that look genuinely hard, write the partial result to proof_strategy.md as 'this remains open; here is what was ruled out'. Then call proof_session_end with reason='converged on partial result' so the loop registers it as a kept partial-result record.
taking Q1: write Section 1 Setup in proof_strategy.md
